outlets.name: CNN
outlets.headline: Oil crashes as Axios reports US-Iran peace deal framework imminent
outlets.summary: CNN covers Araghchi-Wang Yi Beijing meeting alongside Axios MOU report. Oil crashing 9-11%. China calls war 'illegitimate.' Framing: Trump paused Project Freedom, now scrambling for deal while Pezeshkian called terms 'impossible' hours earlier.

outlets.name: Washington Post
outlets.headline: Trump keeps changing his timeline for ending the Iran war
outlets.summary: WaPo focuses on Trump's shifting strategy — from launching Project Freedom to pausing it within 24 hours. Pattern of escalation-then-reversal. Iran's Pezeshkian calls US terms 'impossible.'

outlets.name: NPR
outlets.headline: U.S. says the Iran ceasefire holds despite attacks in Strait of Hormuz and against UAE
outlets.summary: NPR carries Hegseth Pentagon briefing — 'ceasefire is not over,' 6 more blockade-runners stopped. Covers second-day UAE attacks, Oman strike, and Trump's Vatican rift. Notes oil prices easing as US says ceasefire holds.

outlets.name: Washington Post
outlets.headline: Satellite images reveal Iran damaged 228 structures at U.S. bases — far more than acknowledged
outlets.summary: WaPo investigation finds Iranian airstrikes damaged or destroyed at least 228 structures across US Middle East bases since Feb 28. Hangars, barracks, fuel depots, aircraft, radars, and air defense systems hit — far higher than Pentagon publicly acknowledged. Underscores extent of Iran's missile/drone reach during Operation Epic Fury.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: US and Iran closing in on framework deal to end war — oil prices plunge
outlets.summary: Fox frames MOU progress as validation of Trump's deal-making. Oil down 9-11%. Emphasizes Trump's threat that 'bombing starts at much higher level' if Iran doesn't agree. AAA gas prices soar 33 cents in one week to $4.48.

outlets.name: Daily Caller
outlets.headline: US Destroys Six Iranian Ships In Strait Of Hormuz During 'Project Freedom'
outlets.summary: Daily Caller emphasizes US military dominance — 6 boats destroyed, all threats neutralized. Frames as vindication of Trump's aggressive approach to reopening the strait.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: US intercepts 6 ships trying to run American blockade, Hegseth says
outlets.summary: Fox covers Hegseth Pentagon briefing — 6 more blockade-runners stopped, US 'locked and loaded.' Frames Project Freedom as decisive Trump leadership keeping the seas open.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: 'Operation Epic Fury' has ended: Is the Iran war over?
outlets.summary: Al Jazeera analyzes whether the war is truly ending. Rubio declared Epic Fury 'over,' Axios reports MOU imminent, oil crashes. But Pezeshkian called US terms 'impossible,' IRGC acted without government knowledge in UAE attacks, and ships still being attacked in Hormuz.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: Iran says US military killed five civilians in attacks on passenger boats
outlets.summary: Covers Iran's counter-narrative claiming US attacked civilian boats from Khasab (Oman), not IRGC fast boats. 5 civilians allegedly killed. CENTCOM denies. Competing claims unresolved.

outlets.name: Bloomberg
outlets.headline: Iran's Foreign Minister Heads to China for First Time Since War
outlets.summary: Bloomberg covers Araghchi's first Beijing visit since the war began. Talks with FM Wang Yi on bilateral relations and regional developments amid Project Freedom escalation.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: US and Iran said closing in on framework to end war, start 30 days of nuclear talks
outlets.summary: ToI leads with Axios MOU exclusive. Framework includes enrichment moratorium, sanctions relief, Hormuz reopening. Oil crashes. Netanyahu's criminal trial testimony ended early. 2 IDF soldiers wounded by Hezbollah drone.

outlets.name: Haaretz
outlets.headline: Don't Downplay the Risk: Iran and Trump Are One Step Away From All-out Regional War
outlets.summary: Haaretz editorial warns ceasefire is collapsing and full regional war is imminent. Critical of both Trump's escalatory rhetoric and Israel's strike planning.

outlets.name: Jerusalem Post
outlets.headline: IDF tries to assassinate Hezbollah Radwan commander in Beirut in first attack on capital in weeks
outlets.summary: JPost reports IDF strike on Haret Hreik in Beirut's southern Dahieh suburb killed Malek Ballout, operations commander of Hezbollah's elite Radwan Force. First Israeli strike on Beirut since the April 16 ceasefire — major escalation amid US-Iran peace talks. Netanyahu convened security cabinet, plans to speak with Trump tonight.

outlets.name: Iran International
outlets.headline: Pezeshkian brands IRGC escalation 'madness' as tensions rise in Tehran
outlets.summary: Iran International leads with internal power struggle — Pezeshkian calling IRGC UAE strikes 'completely irresponsible.' Reveals deep rift between civilian government and military. IRGC acted without government knowledge.

outlets.name: Iran International
outlets.headline: Iran's labor market cracking under layoffs and inflation
outlets.summary: Reports on Iran's deteriorating economy — mass layoffs, inflation, Supreme Leader's call for 'economic jihad.' Blockade causing severe domestic economic pain. Khamenei asks business owners to avoid layoffs.

outlets.name: Press TV
outlets.headline: Iran activates new Strait of Hormuz transit system as US blockade ends in failure
outlets.summary: Press TV frames PGSA launch as Iranian sovereignty triumph. Claims Project Freedom pause proves US failure. IRGC warns ships outside Iranian corridor face 'decisive action.'
